name: a neuronal organoidthat can be grown from my own cells via ipsc and is ultimately able to interface with me via my pc

phase_0.domain_name: a neuronal organoidthat can be grown from my own cells via ipsc and is ultimately able to interface with me via my pc
phase_0.boundary_condition: closed cultivation chamber with controlled environment

VINIT.justification: initial void state before any transformation begins, raw material for the process

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and regulates the entire organoid development system

AFWD.justification: directed transformation from ipsc toward mature neuronal tissue

AREV.justification: reverse transformation capability for error correction or developmental adjustment

CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of morphogenetic signals to connect developmental stages

IMSCRIB.justification: the neural network's capacity for self-reference and internal state awareness

FSPLIT.justification: cellular differentiation into distinct neuronal subtypes (motor, sensory, interneurons)

FFUSE.justification: reconstitution of unified organoid from specialized differentiated components

EVALT.justification: positive evaluation state where organoid develops functional connectivity

EVALF.justification: negative evaluation state where organoid fails to establish proper neural networks

ENGAGR.justification: simultaneous presence of both excitatory and inhibitory pathways maintaining dynamic equilibrium

IFIX.justification: permanent molecular record of the original patient cell identity

phase_3.void_description: patient cells in quiescent state before reprogramming, no neural commitment established
phase_3.true_description: organoid exhibiting spontaneous electrical activity and response to stimuli
phase_3.false_description: organoid remaining silent with no detectable neural activity patterns
phase_3.both_description: organoid displaying both synchronized bursting and individual firing patterns simultaneously

steps.domain_action: patient somatic cells harvested and prepared for reprogramming
genetic identity tagged with patient-specific markers
cellular reprogramming initiated toward induced pluripotent state
pluripotency factors integrated and validated
pluripotent cells directed toward neural ectoderm specification
neural induction signals activated and maintained
neural progenitors specified into distinct regional fates
organoid formation through 3D culture aggregation
extracellular matrix components incorporated for structural integrity
neural differentiation branches into glutamatergic and GABAergic populations
excitatory and inhibitory neurons integrate into functional circuits
synaptic network formation and maturation
intrinsic electrical activity emerges and stabilizes
cultivation chamber maintains sterile conditions throughout
neural activity monitored and calibrated
sensory and motor regions develop distinct connectivity profiles
integrated organoid exhibits coordinated neural responses
balance between excitation and inhibition maintained dynamically
interface hardware connection established
bioelectronic signal transduction protocols implemented
successful bidirectional communication achieved between organoid and PC
baseline neural activity patterns permanently recorded
system operates within stable homeostatic parameters
organoid recognizes and responds to PC interaction patterns

phase_5.compiler_frontend: biological master regulators that translate genetic programs into cellular behaviors
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: electrochemical synapses and gap junctions enabling cell-to-cell communication
phase_5.memory_mechanism: epigenetic modifications and long-term potentiation mechanisms storing learned states
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: cell-autonomous circadian clocks and mitotic timing circuits
phase_5.alfs_store: the human genome and associated epigenetic reference structures

phase_6.cycle_cost: ATP consumption and metabolic resources for cellular maintenance and growth
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: quiescent stem cells in defined media
phase_6.post_cycle_state: active neural organoid with electrical activity and interface capability
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — energy invested in organized neural structure offsets entropy increase through structured information processing
